message:
test(fcp-store, fcp-streaming): repair three pre-existing test failures

Three tests were failing on main before this session — flagged across
multiple prior sessions (`ywpup`, `fm746`, `s5u65`, `aof5n` per the
4ce27ac3 commit message; April-23 `7bb4b582` for the websocket one) but
never repaired. Each one is a stale test expectation against production
behavior that legitimately changed:

1. `fcp-streaming::websocket::tests::ws_connection_recv_times_out_when_peer_blackholes_after_handshake`

   The test set `pong_timeout = 50ms` and asserted recv() would time
   out at exactly 50ms when the peer blackholes for 250ms. But the
   steady-state I/O budget is computed by `WsConfig::io_timeout()` as
   `connect_timeout.max(ping_interval + pong_timeout)` — and
   `ping_interval` defaults to `Some(30s)`, which the test never
   touched. Effective recv timeout was therefore ~30s, the server
   thread closed TCP at 250ms first, and recv returned `Ok(None)`
   (clean close) rather than the expected `Err(Timeout(50ms))`.

   Fix: explicitly set `ping_interval = None` and a tighter
   `connect_timeout = 100ms` in the test config, and assert on the
   actual effective timeout `connect_timeout.max(pong_timeout) = 100ms`
   rather than `pong_timeout` alone. Doc-comment on the test names
   each input that has to be set and why, so a future reader does not
   re-introduce the same accidental swamping. Server stall extended
   to 500ms so the timeout window is comfortably below TCP-close.

   Production code (`io_timeout`) intentionally NOT changed — the
   `connect_timeout` floor is a conservative safety bound for I/O on
   already-connected sockets, and removing it would change behavior
   for callers who configured a generous connect window expecting it
   to also cover slow steady-state writes.

2. `fcp-store::quarantine::tests::evict_expired`
3. `fcp-store::quarantine::tests::evict_expired_all_expired`

   Both tests were written before commit `0f1f4478a` (April 22) added
   an in-band sweep at `QuarantineStore::quarantine()`: every
   admission now first calls `self.evict_expired(obj.received_at)`
   before inserting. The tests space `received_at` values further
   apart than the TTL, so during the third `quarantine()` call the
   in-band sweep silently expires the first object — and the explicit
   `evict_expired(N)` call afterwards counts only the residual.

   Test 1 expected `evicted == 2`, actual = 1 (id=1 already swept).
   Test 2 expected `evicted == 3`, actual = 2 (id=1 already swept).

   Fix: pack `received_at` values within the TTL window of each
   other so quarantine()'s in-band sweep cannot pre-empt any prior
   object before the next is admitted. Comment names the
   pre-emption hazard so the spacing constraint survives future
   refactors. Test invariants ("evict_expired sweeps expired
   objects") are preserved — only the setup that lets quarantine()
   run cleanly is adjusted.

   Production code NOT changed — the in-band sweep is correct and
   wanted; only the test setup that mis-modeled it needed repair.

Verified across all three crates:
  cargo test -p fcp-streaming --lib  →  529/529 pass
  cargo test -p fcp-store --lib      →  810/810 pass
  cargo test -p fcp-oauth --lib      →  623/623 pass  (carries earlier
                                                       redact-test fixes)
